tagline: Enhance security and flexibility with OAuth connections.
what_it_does: This update allows users to authenticate Databricks connections in Microsoft Power Platform using OAuth instead of personal access tokens (PAT). It requires unified login or SSO to be enabled and supports creating Power canvas apps, Power Automate flows, and Copilot Studio agents. The integration enhances security by providing a more robust authentication mechanism.
why_it_matters: By enabling OAuth authentication, organizations can improve security and compliance while reducing the risk associated with managing personal access tokens. This flexibility benefits data teams and developers by streamlining the connection process to Databricks, ultimately enhancing productivity and data governance.
plain_summary: This update allows users to authenticate Databricks connections in Microsoft Power Platform using OAuth instead of personal access tokens (PAT). It requires unified login or SSO to be enabled and supports creating Power canvas apps, Power Automate flows, and…

capabilities.title: OAuth Authentication
capabilities.desc: Authenticate Databricks connections using OAuth for enhanced security.

capabilities.title: Power Platform Integration
capabilities.desc: Seamlessly connect Databricks with Power Apps and Power Automate.

capabilities.title: Unified Login Requirement
capabilities.desc: Requires unified login or SSO to be enabled for OAuth authentication.

capabilities.title: Data Policy Management
capabilities.desc: Add Databricks to Business data policies to protect data sharing.

prerequisites: Unified login or SSO must be enabled.
Premium Power Apps license is required.
Databricks SQL access with CAN USE permission on a SQL warehouse.
use_cases: Creating Power canvas apps using Databricks data.
Automating workflows with Power Automate that access Databricks.
Integrating Databricks with Copilot Studio for enhanced data insights.
limitations: The Power Platform connector does not support government clouds.

steps.title: Access Connections
steps.desc: In Power Apps or Power Automate, click on 'Connections' from the sidebar.

steps.title: Create New Connection
steps.desc: Click '+ New connection' and search for 'Databricks'.

steps.title: Select Authentication Method
steps.desc: Choose 'AWS OAuth Connection' for OAuth or enter your PAT.

steps.title: Enter Connection Details
steps.desc: Provide the server hostname and HTTP path for the SQL warehouse.

steps.title: Finalize Connection
steps.desc: Click 'Create' and sign in to your Databricks account.

key_takeaway: OAuth authentication for Databricks in Power Platform enhances security and simplifies connection management.
